exampleSentencesTranslated: He has to take advantage of his free time to study English every day.
She takes advantage of her lunch break to call her family.
People usually take advantage of big sales to go shopping.
notes:
tranh thủ = to take advantage of a chance, especially to use a short free moment to do something useful.

It’s a verb phrase. You use it when time or opportunity is limited, and you want to make good use of it. Often followed by thời gian / cơ hội / lúc + rảnh or directly by another verb.

It often implies “before it’s gone” — a bit of urgency. Close in meaning to tận dụng, but tranh thủ feels more about short moments or quick chances.
exampleSentences: Anh phải tranh thủ thời gian rảnh để học tiếng Anh mỗi ngày.
Chị ấy tranh thủ nghỉ trưa để gọi điện về cho gia đình.
Mọi người thường tranh thủ mua sắm khi có giảm giá lớn.

It’s a verb phrase. You use it when time or opportunity is limited, and you want to make good use of it. Often followed by **thời gian / cơ hội / lúc + rảnh** or directly by another verb.


It often implies “before it’s gone” — a bit of urgency. Close in meaning to **tận dụng**, but *tranh thủ* feels more about short moments or quick chances.
